proof_trace: Memory leak: ctx allocated via damon_new_ctx() on L434 is never freed when damon_select_ops() fails on L438, causing direct return -EINVAL on L439. Other error paths properly call damon_destroy_ctx().
attack_scenario: Module loading triggers memory leak when damon_select_ops fails, leaking kernel memory allocated by kzalloc in damon_new_ctx.

proof_trace: Function only frees main struct via kfree() but never frees target->regions allocated in add_dirs. When kobject refcount drops to zero through sysfs operations, release is called directly bypassing rm_dirs cleanup.
attack_scenario: Attacker triggers sysfs operations causing kobject reference drops, leading to release function calls that leak target->regions allocations, eventually exhausting kernel memory.
reason: Missing kfree for target->regions in release

proof_trace: The release function only frees the main struct but not the dynamically allocated targets_arr field. The error path in damon_sysfs_context_add_dirs() (line 1798) calls kobject_put() without prior cleanup, causing a memory leak when targets_arr was allocated via nr_targets_store().
attack_scenario: Attacker writes to /sys/.../nr_targets to trigger targets_arr allocation, then causes damon_sysfs_context_set_schemes() to fail, triggering the error path that leaks the allocated memory.
reason: Missing kfree for targets_arr in release

proof_trace: First allocation succeeds and is added to context (line 396), but if second allocation fails (line 399-401), function returns -ENOMEM without freeing the first scheme. Memory leak occurs because first scheme remains in context without cleanup.
attack_scenario: Attacker triggers memory pressure during second allocation by consuming available memory before module parameter store operation, causing repeated scheme structure leaks.
reason: Leak when second damos allocation fails
